items.q: How long does the ATP-PC system last at maximum intensity?
items.why: Students overestimate the ATP-PC system. It provides instant energy but stores deplete in ~10 seconds (one sprint, one heavy lift). After that, glycolysis or aerobic systems take over.
Students confuse storage with circulation. Blood carries glucose; muscles and liver store glycogen (the storage form). Liver glycogen regulates blood sugar; muscle glycogen fuels local contraction.

items.q: What does the 'M' in SMART goals stand for?
items.why: Students guess from common psychology terms. SMART = Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Realistic (or Relevant), Time-bound. Measurable means progress can be tracked (e.g., '5 km in 25 minutes' not 'get faster').

items.q: How many players in a football team?
items.why: Correct answer: 11. “15” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Correct answer: The goalkeeper. “The forward” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Standard football match length in minutes (without injury time)?
items.why: Exact answer: 90. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
